title: Fix Disable User Status Not Reflecting in UI
story: As a product administrator, I want the user management UI to immediately reflect when I disable a user, so that I can confirm the action was successful and have the option to re-enable them.
description:
After disabling a user in production, the page refreshed but the UX did not update to show the user as disabled and did not provide a re-enable option.

Root cause: MySQL enum type with numeric values ('0', '1') was being set with integer values (0, 1) instead of string values. MySQL treats enum values as strings, so integer inserts resulted in empty strings. This worked in PostgreSQL but not MySQL.
acceptance_criteria: Disabling a user immediately reflects the disabled state in the UI
Re-enable option is visible for disabled users
Fix works consistently across all environments (dev, staging, production)
ux_changes: User status now correctly reflects disabled state after toggling. Re-enable option is visible.
middleware_changes: Fixed enum value assignment to use string values ('0', '1') instead of integers for MySQL compatibility.
data_model_changes: team-user status enum field value handling corrected for MySQL string-based enum behavior.
implementation_notes: MySQL enum fields with numeric values must be set using strings ('0', '1') not integers. PostgreSQL handled integers correctly, masking the bug during development.
comment_summary: Developer identified MySQL enum string handling issue. Fixed on all three environments.
